mechanism_narrative: The steady month-over-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of PAYEMS changes on 20-day forward returns is non-positive over any rolling 2-year period, or the signal does not consistently predict positive returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals that the labor market is tightening, which historically precedes increased consumer spending and equity market gains over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE drops below 8.0%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the mean excess return over baseline is not significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: The combined acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based demand recovery, where the interaction of supply and residential investment growth amplifies the positive effect on equity valuations beyond the sum of individual components.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and HOUST has a Rank IC of zero or negative over any 3-year window, or the joint signal does not produce higher information coefficients than the standalone series.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is elevated (UNRATE > 8.5%), payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) has an outsized positive impact on S&P 500 returns as it signals that the recovery is durable and broad-based, reducing recession fears.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 8.5% is not statistically different from the correlation when UNRATE <= 8.5% at the 5% level, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in at least 55% of high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals rising inflation pressures, which lead to expectations of tighter monetary policy, higher discount rates, and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of CPIAUCSL changes on 60-day forward returns is consistently positive over a 3-year rolling window, contradicting the negative directional prediction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) above a threshold quickly boosts market sentiment and near-term earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: After RSAFS growth exceeds 1%, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is negative or zero in more than 45% of cases, or the average return is not significantly greater than zero at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) both increase month-over-month, it indicates synchronized expansion in manufacturing and consumer demand, reinforcing growth expectations and leading to positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
regime_hint: early cyclical recovery with supply-demand alignment
failure_condition: The joint indicator (both series positive) does not predict positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term is not significant in a regression of forward returns on the two series and their product.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS below 2%), increases in housing starts (HOUST) are particularly potent signals of economic recovery because low rates amplify the stimulative effect on construction activity and related sectors, boosting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 2% is not significantly positive, or is weaker than the correlation when FEDFUNDS >= 2% at the 10% significance level.
